children.heading: Qualified individuals
children.label: 1 Qualified individuals
children.text: A qualified individual may enroll in any qualified health plan available to such individual and for which such individual is eligible.

children.heading: Employer may specify level
children.label: A Employer may specify level
children.text: A qualified employer may provide support for coverage of employees under a qualified health plan by selecting any level of coverage under to be made available to employees through an Exchange.section 18022(d) of this title

children.heading: Employee may choose plans within a level
children.label: B Employee may choose plans within a level
children.text: Each employee of a qualified employer that elects a level of coverage under subparagraph (A) may choose to enroll in a qualified health plan that offers coverage at that level.

children.heading: Payment of premiums by qualified individuals
children.label: b Payment of premiums by qualified individuals
children.text: A qualified individual enrolled in any qualified health plan may pay any applicable premium owed by such individual to the health insurance issuer issuing such qualified health plan.

children.heading: Individual market
children.label: 1 Individual market
children.text: A health insurance issuer shall consider all enrollees in all health plans (other than grandfathered health plans) offered by such issuer in the individual market, including those enrollees who do not enroll in such plans through the Exchange, to be members of a single risk pool.

children.heading: Small group market
children.label: 2 Small group market
children.text: A health insurance issuer shall consider all enrollees in all health plans (other than grandfathered health plans) offered by such issuer in the small group market, including those enrollees who do not enroll in such plans through the Exchange, to be members of a single risk pool.

children.heading: Merger of markets
children.label: 3 Merger of markets
children.text: A State may require the individual and small group insurance markets within a State to be merged if the State determines appropriate.

children.heading: State law
children.label: 4 State law
children.text: A State law requiring grandfathered health plans to be included in a pool described in paragraph (1) or (2) shall not apply.

children.heading: Continued operation of market outside Exchanges
children.label: 1 Continued operation of market outside Exchanges
A
children.text: a health insurance issuer from offering outside of an Exchange a health plan to a qualified individual or qualified employer; and

children.label: B
children.text: a qualified individual from enrolling in, or a qualified employer from selecting for its employees, a health plan offered outside of an Exchange.

children.heading: Continued operation of State benefit requirements
children.label: 2 Continued operation of State benefit requirements
children.text: Nothing in this title shall be construed to terminate, abridge, or limit the operation of any requirement under State law with respect to any policy or plan that is offered outside of an Exchange to offer benefits.1

children.heading: Choice to enroll or not to enroll
children.label: A Choice to enroll or not to enroll
children.text: Nothing in this title shall be construed to restrict the choice of a qualified individual to enroll or not to enroll in a qualified health plan or to participate in an Exchange.1

children.heading: Prohibition against compelled enrollment
children.label: B Prohibition against compelled enrollment
children.text: Nothing in this title shall be construed to compel an individual to enroll in a qualified health plan or to participate in an Exchange.1

children.heading: Individuals allowed to enroll in any plan
children.label: C Individuals allowed to enroll in any plan
children.text: A qualified individual may enroll in any qualified health plan, except that in the case of a catastrophic plan described in , a qualified individual may enroll in the plan only if the individual is eligible to enroll in the plan under .section 18022(e) of this titlesection 18022(e)(2) of this title

children.heading: Members of Congress in the Exchange
children.label: D Members of Congress in the Exchange
I
children.text: created under this Act (or an amendment made by this Act); or

children.label: II
children.text: offered through an Exchange established under this Act (or an amendment made by this Act).

children.heading: Member of Congress
children.label: I Member of Congress
children.text: The term “Member of Congress” means any member of the House of Representatives or the Senate.

children.heading: Congressional staff
children.label: II Congressional staff
children.text: The term “congressional staff” means all full-time and part-time employees employed by the official office of a Member of Congress, whether in Washington, DC or outside of Washington, DC.

children.heading: No penalty for transferring to minimum essential coverage outside Exchange
children.label: 4 No penalty for transferring to minimum essential coverage outside Exchange
children.text: An Exchange, or a qualified health plan offered through an Exchange, shall not impose any penalty or other fee on an individual who cancels enrollment in a plan because the individual becomes eligible for minimum essential coverage (as defined in without regard to paragraph (1)(C) or (D) thereof) or such coverage becomes affordable (within the meaning of section 36B(c)(2)(C) of such title).section 5000A(f) of title 26

children.heading: Enrollment through agents or brokers
children.label: e Enrollment through agents or brokers

children.text: to enroll individuals and employers in any qualified health plans in the individual or small group market as soon as the plan is offered through an Exchange in the State; and
to assist individuals in applying for premium tax credits and cost-sharing reductions for plans sold through an Exchange.

children.heading: Qualified individuals and employers; access limited to citizens and lawful residents
children.label: f Qualified individuals and employers; access limited to citizens and lawful residents
i
children.text: is seeking to enroll in a qualified health plan in the individual market offered through the Exchange; and

children.label: ii
children.text: resides in the State that established the Exchange.

children.heading: Incarcerated individuals excluded
children.label: B Incarcerated individuals excluded
children.text: An individual shall not be treated as a qualified individual if, at the time of enrollment, the individual is incarcerated, other than incarceration pending the disposition of charges.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “qualified employer” means a small employer that elects to make all full-time employees of such employer eligible for 1 or more qualified health plans offered in the small group market through an Exchange that offers qualified health plans.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Beginning in 2017, each State may allow issuers of health insurance coverage in the large group market in the State to offer qualified health plans in such market through an Exchange. Nothing in this subparagraph shall be construed as requiring the issuer to offer such plans through an Exchange.

children.heading: Large employers eligible
children.label: ii Large employers eligible
children.text: If a State under clause (i) allows issuers to offer qualified health plans in the large group market through an Exchange, the term “qualified employer” shall include a large employer that elects to make all full-time employees of such employer eligible for 1 or more qualified health plans offered in the large group market through the Exchange.

children.heading: Access limited to lawful residents
children.label: 3 Access limited to lawful residents
children.text: If an individual is not, or is not reasonably expected to be for the entire period for which enrollment is sought, a citizen or national of the United States or an alien lawfully present in the United States, the individual shall not be treated as a qualified individual and may not be covered under a qualified health plan in the individual market that is offered through an Exchange.
